spec_match.rationale: Exercises the round <length> syntax directly with a radius slider against a chosen shape — closest to a 'spec sandbox' of the polygon cluster.

interactive.rationale: Shape buttons + radius slider + live preview + live CSS readout, all driving real clip-path changes.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Promises a shape picker and corner radius adjustment; delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Radius slider shows tightest effective radius and clamped vertex count, and the page now probes the exact syntax boundary: polygon(round <length>, ...) versus rejected polygon(round <percentage>, ...). Animation, offset-path, shape-outside, and fill-rule combinations are covered by sibling concept pages linked from this explorer.

design_system.rationale: Support banner uses --accent-emerald / --accent-rose, control panels use --bg-paper/--border-black, no static-card, shape buttons override background and color together.

summary: The shape picker and radius slider expose requested radius, tightest effective radius, and clamped vertex count. The new syntax-boundary panel probes the real clip-path contract and shows that round <length> is the valid slot while round <percentage> is rejected; sibling concepts cover animation, offset-path, shape-outside, and fill-rule contexts.
